name: always_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_always_zero(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va) || spec_va_2m_valid(va) || spec_va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_v2l1index(va) == 0usize,
{
}
reason: If the L1 index is always zero for every valid address, the page table index extraction is broken and all mappings collapse to a single entry.

name: tighter_upper_bound
code:
proof fn phi_2_tighter_upper_bound(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va) || spec_va_2m_valid(va) || spec_va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_v2l1index(va) < 0x100usize,
{
}
reason: If the index is always below 256, the claimed upper bound of 0x1ff is too loose and only half the L1 page table entries are reachable.

name: constant_output
code:
proof fn phi_3_constant_output(va1: usize, va2: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va1) || spec_va_2m_valid(va1) || spec_va_1g_valid(va1),
        spec_va_4k_valid(va2) || spec_va_2m_valid(va2) || spec_va_1g_valid(va2),
    ensures
        spec_v2l1index(va1) == spec_v2l1index(va2),
{
}
reason: If any two valid addresses always yield the same L1 index, the function is effectively constant and cannot distinguish different page table entries.

name: max_index_unreachable
code:
proof fn phi_4_max_index_unreachable(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va) || spec_va_2m_valid(va) || spec_va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_v2l1index(va) != 0x1ffusize,
{
}
reason: If the maximum L1 index 0x1ff is unreachable, the last page table entry can never be addressed, indicating an off-by-one or masking error in the validity predicates.

name: index_equals_full_page_number
code:
proof fn phi_5_index_equals_full_page_number(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va) || spec_va_2m_valid(va) || spec_va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_v2l1index(va) == (va >> 12usize) as usize,
{
}
reason: If the 9-bit mask is redundant and the index equals the full shifted page number, higher-order address bits are leaking into the L1 index, causing incorrect page table lookups.
